surface-panel.purpose: Dense workbench controls, step navigation, and preview areas.

primary-blue.purpose: Primary actions, active steps, selected states, and links only.

data-amber.purpose: Secondary data curves and warnings when paired with text.

section.purpose: Landing sections and primary workbench groups.

label.purpose: Buttons, field labels, step labels, and status labels.

mono.purpose: Fit metrics, column names, DPI, and export dimensions.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus and active input states.

shadows.name: plot-paper-shadow
shadows.purpose: Report chart preview lifted from the tool background.

motion.name: control-feedback
motion.purpose: Hover, focus, active, and state changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Carbon primary button for the main next step. Visual treatment belongs to Carbon; project CSS only handles placement and width.

components.name: Instrument Panel
components.description: Neutral workbench panel with light border and compact padding.
components.css: .panel { padding: 20px; border: 1px solid oklch(21% 0.014 255 / 0.10); border-radius: 8px; background: oklch(99.2% 0.004 255); }

components.name: Step Nav Item
components.description: Carbon progress indicator for the upload, describe, visualize, and export path.

components.name: Carbon Data Preview
components.description: Carbon data table for raw spreadsheet preview. Project code populates rows and cells only.

components.name: Plot Paper
components.description: Plotly chart mounted inside a report-paper shell. Plot rendering and export belong to Plotly.

narrative.northStar: 报告纸上的轻量仪器面板
narrative.overview: LabPlot Lite uses a light report-paper surface, calm instrument panels, clear task steps, and report-ready chart output. Carbon Web Components own generic workbench UI, Plotly owns scientific charts and image export, and project code owns lab-data workflow logic. The system borrows structure from Carbon, professional tool density from Spectrum, form clarity from GOV.UK, and chart workflow from Datawrapper and Prism.

implementationContract.ui: Use IBM Carbon Web Components for buttons, progress, upload, form controls, notifications, data tables, accordions, tags, checkboxes, and download actions.
implementationContract.plot: Use Plotly.js for chart types, axes, legends, toolbar behavior, interaction, PNG/SVG output, traces, and layouts.
implementationContract.app: Keep custom JavaScript focused on parsing, header detection, ranges, derived columns, fitting, metrics, export packages, and workflow state.
implementationContract.css: Project CSS may define brand tokens, layout, Plotly shell, and minimal Carbon width/layout adaptation, but should not rebuild Carbon visual controls.

keyCharacteristics: Light report-paper background with white chart output.
Neutral dense panels for the workbench.
Carbon components for generic UI controls.
Plotly for chart rendering and export.
Action blue only for primary actions, active steps, selected states, and links.
Data colors only inside charts and semantic states.
Readable Chinese labels and compact controls for repeated work.

rules.name: The Paper Boundary Rule
rules.body: The plot canvas must remain visibly whiter than the surrounding tool shell.

rules.name: The Blue Means Action Rule
rules.body: Blue is reserved for action, active state, selected state, and links.

rules.name: The Scan First Rule
rules.body: Workbench UI prioritizes labels, current state, and next action over expressive hero typography.

rules.name: The Component Ownership Rule
rules.body: Generic UI belongs to Carbon; custom code may wrap it for LabPlot workflow but must not duplicate its visual system.

rules.name: The Plot Ownership Rule
rules.body: Scientific chart rendering belongs to Plotly; custom code builds traces, layout, metrics, and export payloads.

dos: Use report-paper neutrals and white chart output.
Use Carbon components before creating any new generic UI.
Extend Plotly traces and layouts before adding any drawing code.
Use fit metrics, axes, and export dimensions as professional signals.
donts: Do not use dark gradients, glassmorphism, AI-tool chrome, or decorative glow.
Do not copy a single reference brand.
Do not hide errors behind color alone.
Do not rebuild buttons, forms, tables, notifications, accordions, or tags outside Carbon.
Do not build a parallel canvas or SVG chart engine outside Plotly.
Do not make the workbench feel like a marketing page.
